prompt: How many integers n with 1 <= n < 1,000,000 can be written as n = a^2 + b^2 where a and b are both integers >= 1? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth by direct enumeration = 215,907 (verify_answers.py). Differential shape: the Landau-Ramanujan leading term K*n/sqrt(ln n) anchors ~205k, ~5% low (the secondary term lifts it) -- a tight anchor whose small bias punishes narrow intervals; note a,b >= 1 differs from the classical a,b >= 0 count.
